$comment: Mode definitions for the winning-writing skill. Each mode names which rule tags apply, the target length, and any per-rule severity overrides for this mode. Adding a new mode means: pick a name, set the target/severity, and tag rules in catalog.json with the new mode.

default.label: Default (all rules)
default.description: No filter. Every rule in catalog.json fires. Use when you don't know which mode applies or when you want a maximalist critique.

cold-email.label: Cold email
cold-email.description: Unsolicited message to someone with more power than the sender. The 10 rules of cold outreach, plus zero-tolerance on AI tells (em-dash, consultant jargon, generic openers). Under 200 words.

memo.label: Exec memo / decision memo
memo.description: Document going up the chain to ask for a decision, surface a status, or defend a recommendation. The reader has 60 seconds. TL;DR up front, one ask per memo, sourced numbers, named decision criterion, early-failure signal.

essay.label: Personal essay / op-ed
essay.description: First-person narrative. Show-don't-tell, character arc not resume, earned emotional moments. Fragments and hedges OK in this mode.

profile.label: Bio / About section / LinkedIn About
profile.description: Plain technical feature list, semicolon-separated. No competitor names, market positioning, internal framing, moats, or tier language. Says what's true and stops.

readme.label: Project README
readme.description: Plain feature list. No market positioning, tier framing, moat language, or competitor mentions. Describes what the project does. Same voice as profile.

linkedin-post.label: LinkedIn post / long-form
linkedin-post.description: Public post. Aggressive on engagement-bait and corporate jargon; medium on length (over 200 is fine for thought leadership, over 600 is bloat).
